title: There's a Stress Gap Between Men and Women
subtitle: 性别压力差异：女性承受的隐形负担

paragraphs.en: "I used to work very hard. I love to create things, grow them and solve problems," said Meng Li, a successful app developer in San Francisco. "I didn't really care about my mind and my body until they decided to go on strike."
paragraphs.zh: "我曾经工作非常努力。我喜欢创造事物、发展壮大它们并解决问题，"旧金山的成功应用开发者Meng Li说道。"直到我的身心决定罢工，我才开始真正关注它们。"

paragraphs.en: Ms. Li said her stress led to sleeplessness. When she did sleep, she experienced "problem-solving dreams," which left her feeling unrested when she woke up. "After I became a first-time mother, I quickly realized I was so busy caring for other people and work that I felt like I'd lost myself," she said.
paragraphs.zh: Li女士说她的压力导致了失眠。即使入睡，她也会做"解决问题的梦"，醒来后依然感到疲惫不堪。"当我第一次成为母亲后，我很快意识到自己忙于照顾他人和工作，感觉完全迷失了自我，"她说。

paragraphs.en: It's a common story — one we frequently ridicule and readily dismiss, for example, by claiming that women tend to complain more than men, despite the growing sum of research that underlines the problem. Women are twice as likely to suffer from severe stress and anxiety as men, according to a 2016 study published in The Journal of Brain & Behavior. The American Psychological Association reports a gender gap year after year showing that women consistently report higher stress levels.
paragraphs.zh: 这是一个常见的故事——我们经常嘲笑并轻易忽视它，比如声称女性比男性更爱抱怨，尽管越来越多的研究证实了这一问题的存在。根据2016年发表在《大脑与行为杂志》上的一项研究，女性患严重压力和焦虑症的可能性是男性的两倍。美国心理学会年复一年的报告显示，女性的压力水平始终高于男性。

paragraphs.en: Clearly, a stress gap exists. "The difference is not really news to me, as a clinical psychologist," said Erin Joyce, a women and couples therapist in Los Angeles. "It's been well documented in extensive research over the years that prevalence rates for the majority of the anxiety disorders are higher in women than men."
paragraphs.zh: 显然，压力差距确实存在。"作为一名临床心理学家，这种差异对我来说并不新鲜，"洛杉矶的女性与夫妻治疗师Erin Joyce说道。"多年来的大量研究已充分证明，大多数焦虑症的发病率在女性中高于男性。"

paragraphs.en: Some people may argue that this is merely reported data, and they say many men feel the same pressures as women in terms of fulfilling responsibilities at work and home. In other words, we're all really, really stressed.
paragraphs.zh: 有些人可能会辩称这仅仅是报告数据，他们认为许多男性在履行工作和家庭责任方面承受着与女性同样的压力。换句话说，我们所有人的压力都非常、非常大。

paragraphs.en: "The difference, however, is in the nature and scope of these responsibilities in the home environment in particular," Dr. Joyce said. For example, the United Nations reported that women do nearly three times as much unpaid domestic work as men. The problem is, housework is often overlooked as work, even though it is often as laborious, or in some cases more so, as any paid job.
paragraphs.zh: "然而，差异在于这些责任的性质和范围，尤其是在家庭环境中，"Joyce博士说。例如，联合国报告指出，女性承担的无偿家务劳动几乎是男性的三倍。问题在于，家务劳动常常不被视为工作，尽管它往往和任何有偿工作一样辛苦，甚至在某些情况下更为繁重。

paragraphs.en: As the scholar Silvia Federici put it in 1975, the unpaid nature of domestic work reinforces the assumption that "housework is not work, thus preventing women from struggling against it." It's not just inside the home, though.
paragraphs.zh: 正如学者Silvia Federici在1975年所指出的，家务劳动的无偿性质强化了"家务不是工作"的观念，从而阻止了女性对此进行抗争。然而，这不仅仅局限于家庭内部。

paragraphs.en: Research from Nova Southeastern University found that female managers were more likely than male managers to display "surface acting," or forcing emotions that are not wholly felt. "They expressed optimism, calmness and sympathy even when these were not the emotions that they were actually feeling," the study said.
paragraphs.zh: Nova Southeastern大学的研究发现，女性管理者比男性管理者更容易表现出"表面表演"，即强迫自己表达并非真实感受到的情绪。"她们表现出乐观、冷静和同情，即使这些并不是她们真正的情绪感受，"该研究指出。

paragraphs.en: Surface acting is a prime example of "emotional labor," a concept that the writer Jess Zimmerman made familiar in a 2015 essay. The essay sparked a massive thread on the internet community blog MetaFilter. Hundreds of women spoke up about their own experience with emotional labor: the duties that are expected of them, but go unnoticed.
paragraphs.zh: 表面表演是"情绪劳动"的典型例子，这一概念由作家Jess Zimmerman在2015年的一篇文章中广为人知。这篇文章在互联网社区博客MetaFilter上引发了大量讨论。数百名女性分享了自己的情绪劳动经历：那些被期望承担却不被注意到的责任。

paragraphs.en: These invisible duties become apparent only when you don't do them. Like domestic labor, emotional labor is generally dismissed and not labeled work. But research shows it can be just as exhausting as paid work.
paragraphs.zh: 这些隐形的责任只有在你不做的时候才会被注意到。和家务劳动一样，情绪劳动通常被忽视，不被视为工作。但研究表明，它可能和有偿工作一样令人精疲力竭。

words.meaning: adj. 明显的，显而易见的

paragraphs.en: Emotional labor can lead to difficulty in sleeping and family conflict. Sure, circumstantial stress, like losing a job, may lead to these same issues. But emotional labor is not circumstantial — it's an enduring responsibility based on the socialized gender role of women.
paragraphs.zh: 情绪劳动可能导致睡眠困难和家庭冲突。当然，偶发性压力，比如失业，也可能导致同样的问题。但情绪劳动不是偶发性的——它是基于女性社会化性别角色的一种持久责任。

words.meaning: adj. 偶然的，与情况有关的

paragraphs.en: Like Ms. Li, many women try to manage the added stress to reach what Dr. Joyce said was an unattainable ideal. "Some professional women aim to do it all. They want to reach the top of the corporate ladder and fly like supermom," she said.
paragraphs.zh: 和Li女士一样，许多女性试图应对额外的压力，以达到Joyce博士所说的那个无法实现的理想。"一些职业女性想要做到面面俱到。她们想登上企业阶梯的顶端，同时像超级妈妈一样飞翔，"她说。

words.meaning: adj. 无法实现的，达不到的

paragraphs.en: When women don't reach this ideal, they feel guilty, and even more stressed. After her own struggle with this, Ms. Li took a step back and used her experience to build Sanity & Self, a self-care app and platform for overworked women. "The realizations I had in that process helped me gain insights and ultimately got me ready to integrate self-care into my daily life," she said.
paragraphs.zh: 当女性无法达到这一理想时，她们会感到内疚，压力也会更大。在经历了自己的挣扎之后，Li女士退后一步，利用自己的经验创建了Sanity & Self——一个为过度劳累的女性打造的自我关爱应用和平台。"在这个过程中获得的领悟帮助我获得了洞察力，最终让我准备好将自我关爱融入日常生活，"她说。

paragraphs.en: The stress problem extends beyond mental health when you consider the link between stress, anxiety and heart health. Worse, most of what we know about heart disease comes from studies involving men. However, "there are many reasons to think that it's different in women," Harvard Medical School reported.
paragraphs.zh: 当你考虑到压力、焦虑与心脏健康之间的关联时，压力问题就超出了心理健康的范畴。更糟糕的是，我们对心脏病的大部分认知来自以男性为对象的研究。然而，"有很多理由认为女性的情况有所不同，"哈佛医学院报告指出。

paragraphs.en: For example, women are more likely to experience disturbed sleep, anxiety and unusual fatigue before a heart attack. Stress is so normalized that it is easy for women to shrug off those symptoms as simply the consequences of stress. Many women also do not experience chest pain before a heart attack the way men do, which leads to fewer women discovering problematic heart issues.
paragraphs.zh: 例如，女性在心脏病发作前更容易出现睡眠紊乱、焦虑和异常疲劳。压力已经被如此常态化，以至于女性很容易将这些症状视为压力的正常后果而不以为意。许多女性在心脏病发作前也不会像男性那样出现胸痛，这导致更少的女性发现潜在的心脏问题。

words.meaning: phr. 不以为然，满不在乎

paragraphs.en: Harvard reports that women are "much more likely than men to die within a year of having a heart attack" and "many women say their physicians sometimes don't even recognize the symptoms." The good news is, women are more likely than men to take charge of their stress and manage it, the American Psychological Association reports.
paragraphs.zh: 哈佛大学报告指出，女性"在心脏病发作后一年内死亡的可能性远高于男性"，而且"许多女性表示她们的医生有时甚至无法识别这些症状。"好消息是，美国心理学会报告指出，女性比男性更有可能主动掌控并管理自己的压力。

paragraphs.en: The concept of self-care, at its core, is quite simple. "The basics of adequate sleep, healthy diet and exercise are a good place to start," Dr. Joyce said. "Support from trusted relationships is vital. This includes professional support from various health and wellness providers if stress is becoming increasingly overwhelming."
paragraphs.zh: 自我关爱的核心概念其实很简单。"充足的睡眠、健康的饮食和运动是一个好的起点，"Joyce博士说。"来自值得信赖的人际关系的支持至关重要。如果压力变得越来越难以承受，这也包括来自各类健康和保健服务提供者的专业支持。"

words.meaning: adj. 压倒性的，难以承受的

paragraphs.en: Disconnecting from work and home responsibilities is also obviously important. But it's much easier said than done. It is important to understand what causes your stress in the first place.
paragraphs.zh: 暂时脱离工作和家庭责任显然也很重要。但说起来容易做起来难。首先，了解压力的根源是很重要的。

paragraphs.en: "Get really specific with what's stressing you out," Ms. Li said. "We often chalk up our stress to broad experiences like work. But work stress can take many different forms."
paragraphs.zh: "要非常具体地弄清楚是什么让你感到有压力，"Li女士说。"我们常常把压力笼统地归咎于工作之类的大范畴。但工作压力可以有很多不同的表现形式。"

paragraphs.en: "Is a colleague being disrespectful of your time? Is a boss undermining your day-to-day control over decision making? These are different causes of stress and can benefit from different kinds of self-care."
paragraphs.zh: "是某个同事不尊重你的时间吗？还是老板在削弱你对日常决策的控制权？这些是不同的压力来源，可以从不同的自我关爱方式中获益。"

paragraphs.en: Ideally, your spouse or partner will be supportive, rather than dismissive, of your stress. It is important to talk through these issues before they come to a head. "Women working outside of the home should make an effort to have a conscious conversation with their partners about more equitable sharing of household and family responsibilities," Dr. Joyce said.
paragraphs.zh: 理想情况下，你的配偶或伴侣应该支持你，而不是对你的压力不以为然。在问题爆发之前进行充分的沟通非常重要。"在外工作的女性应该主动与伴侣进行有意识的对话，讨论如何更公平地分担家务和家庭责任，"Joyce博士说。
